project: RequestHunt Skill v2.0.0 — Unified Credits Model
description: Update the RequestHunt skill from v1.0.0 to v2.0.0 to reflect the API's migration from split cached/realtime quotas to a unified credits-based billing system. Update scripts, documentation, and registry entries.

userStories.title: Update get_usage.py for unified credits
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want the usage script to display the current unified credit model so that I see accurate quota information instead of the obsolete cached/realtime split.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/requesthunt/scripts/get_usage.py displays a single '## Credits' section showing used, limit, remaining, and resetsAt from the data.credits response object, instead of separate 'Cached Requests' and 'Realtime Requests' sections.
The script displays the user's tier from data.tier (e.g., 'free' or 'pro').
The script displays rate limit info from data.rateLimit (requestsPerMinute) if present.
The script displays API keys info from data.apiKeys (used/limit) if present.
The error message URL references /settings (not /settings/api).
The --json flag still outputs raw JSON unchanged.
The script uses only Python standard library (no external dependencies).
userStories.notes: The API response shape changed from {data: {cached: {...}, realtime: {...}}} to {data: {tier, credits: {used, limit, remaining, resetsAt, period}, apiKeys: {used, limit}, rateLimit: {requestsPerMinute, used, remaining, resetsAt}}}.

userStories.title: Add --depth parameter to scrape_topic.py
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want to control scrape depth so that I can adjust the breadth of data collection and understand the credit cost.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/requesthunt/scripts/scrape_topic.py accepts a --depth argument (integer, default 1).
The script validates that depth is between 1 and 40, exiting with an error if out of range.
The depth value is included in the POST request body sent to /api/v1/scrape.
The formatted output displays the depth value and estimated credit cost (depth x number of platforms).
The usage display at the bottom shows unified credits (credits.used/credits.limit/credits.remaining) instead of the old realtime quota.
The error message URL references /settings (not /settings/api).
The --json flag still outputs raw JSON unchanged.
The script uses only Python standard library (no external dependencies).
userStories.notes: The API POST /api/v1/scrape now accepts {topic, platforms, depth} in the body. The response usage object changed from {realtime: {used, limit, remaining}} to {credits: {used, limit, remaining}}.

userStories.title: Create check_scrape.py script
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want to check the status of a scrape job so that I know when results are ready or if something failed.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file skills/requesthunt/scripts/check_scrape.py exists.
The script accepts a positional job_id argument and an optional --json flag.
It calls GET /api/v1/scrape/{jobId} with Bearer token authentication.
The formatted output displays: Job ID, Status (pending/running/completed/failed), Topic, Platforms, Depth (if present), Requests Created (if completed), Started At, Completed At (if available).
If the response includes a progress object, the script displays per-platform status (e.g., reddit: done, x: running).
If the status is 'failed', the script displays the error message.
The script follows the same patterns as existing scripts: shebang, docstring, stdlib-only imports, get_api_key() helper, argparse, markdown-formatted output.
The script uses only Python standard library (no external dependencies).
userStories.notes: GET /api/v1/scrape/{jobId} returns {data: {jobId, status, topic, platforms, depth, progress: {reddit?, x?, github?}, requestsCreated, error, startedAt, completedAt}}. Progress values are: pending, running, done, error, skipped.

userStories.title: Update API key URL in remaining scripts
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want the error messages to point to the correct settings page URL.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/requesthunt/scripts/list_requests.py error message references https://requesthunt.com/settings (not /settings/api).
skills/requesthunt/scripts/search_requests.py error message references https://requesthunt.com/settings (not /settings/api).
skills/requesthunt/scripts/get_topics.py error message references https://requesthunt.com/settings (not /settings/api).
No other behavioral changes to these three scripts.
userStories.notes: The /settings/api path goes through unnecessary redirects. The canonical URL is /settings.

userStories.title: Update SKILL.md documentation
userStories.description: As a skill user, I want the skill documentation to reflect the current API capabilities and pricing model.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/requesthunt/SKILL.md prerequisite section references https://requesthunt.com/settings (not /settings/api).
The scrape examples in the Research Workflow section include --depth flag usage.
A new 'Check Scrape Job' command section documents the check_scrape.py script with example usage.

The API Info section at the bottom replaces the old rate limits (1000 cached/500 realtime) with the unified credit model: Free tier (100 credits/month, 10 req/min), Pro tier (2,000 credits/month, 60 req/min), cost per API call (1 credit), cost per scrape (depth x platforms credits).
The Scrape Topic command examples include --depth usage.
All other existing content (research workflow, report template, other commands) remains unchanged.

userStories.title: Bump version and update registry
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the skill version bumped to 2.0.0 and the registry updated so that the new version is discoverable.
acceptanceCriteria: skills/requesthunt/.claude-plugin/plugin.json version field is '2.0.0'.
skills.json requesthunt entry version field is '2.0.0'.
skills.json requesthunt commands array includes the check_scrape.py command.
skills.json requesthunt commands array shows --depth in the scrape_topic.py command.
CHANGELOG.md compatibility matrix shows requesthunt at version 2.0.0.
CHANGELOG.md has a requesthunt [2.0.0] entry under [Unreleased] documenting: breaking credit model change, added --depth parameter, added check_scrape.py, fixed URL references.
CHANGELOG.md Rate Limits section at the bottom is updated from '1000/month cached, 500/month realtime' to unified credits model.
userStories.notes: This is a major version bump because the credit model is a breaking change for anyone parsing the old cached/realtime usage output.
